CONFIGURED-panel review (one of 2; return verdict JSON, write NOTHING). Round 1.
Task: T104 (goals:G6) — "Netns verification: standby-path liveness is bidirectional (I8)". Per Q39 an in-goal VERIFICATION task.
acceptance: New -tags e2e test exists and runs in the netns tier: passes proving bidirectional liveness, OR fails with the failure documented and refiled as a defect linked goals:G6 (test kept as repro). Either outcome satisfies the task per Q39.
Worktree: /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-accde2d95d6ea72dc (branch implement/T104, base 55ff983, HEAD 1d5d01e). Go repo — gate via `nix develop -c sh -c '...'`. Privileged -tags e2e tier NOT runnable here — judge via compile/vet + inspection.
Worker: pass; new file test/e2e/standby_liveness_test.go + Topology.BlockEgress/UnblockEgress (tc clsact matchall drop) in netns.go. Worker predicts subtest (a) idle-standby tx-grows FAILS (emitProbes in internal/bind/probe.go never calls ps.txBytes.Add — reproducing production path_up=1/tx=0) and (b) egress-blocked-standby-goes-DOWN PASSES.
Scrutinize adversarially: (a) is the BlockEgress tc-clsact filter genuinely a ONE-WAY egress block that COEXISTS with the existing netem root qdisc (not clobbering it), and correctly removed by UnblockEgress? (b) does subtest (a)'s tx-growth assertion read the RIGHT counter (wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total via PathSnapshots) and would it actually observe probe emission if probes DID increment tx? (c) INDEPENDENTLY confirm the emitProbes-bypasses-ps.txBytes claim by reading internal/bind/probe.go + multipath.go — is the tx-counter-omission real, and does it mean an idle-but-egress-DEAD standby would show path_up=1 while truly being one-way? (d) does the test compile/vet under -tags e2e and match harness idioms? Given the either-outcome acceptance, is the test a faithful REPRO regardless of pass/fail? Recommend whether to file a G6 defect now on static evidence or defer to the hardware run. Return { taskId, verdict, criticism: [], questions: [], defects: [], rationale, summary }.

diff --git a/test/e2e/netns.go b/test/e2e/netns.go
index 978f639..591f7a3 100644
--- a/test/e2e/netns.go
+++ b/test/e2e/netns.go
@@ -231,6 +231,30 @@ func (top *Topology) Restore(name string) {
 	top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
 }
 
+// BlockEgress drops ALL outbound traffic on the named path's edge veth via a tc
+// clsact egress filter (matchall + drop), while leaving the veth up and inbound
+// traffic untouched — a one-way egress failure (e.g. a dead uplink amplifier), as
+// opposed to Blackhole's bidirectional link-down. It is used by T104 to prove
+// liveness is genuinely bidirectional: with only egress dead the peer's own probes
+// still ARRIVE at this path (an operator naively trusting "receiving traffic" as
+// health would stay wrongly up), but this path's OWN probe/echo round trip cannot
+// complete, so it must go DOWN. clsact attaches its own ingress/egress hooks
+// independent of whatever root qdisc is set, so it coexists with the path's netem
+// root qdisc (delay/jitter/loss) without disturbing it.
+func (top *Topology) BlockEgress(name string) {
+	p := top.path(name)
+	top.run("tc", "qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
+	top.run("tc", "filter", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "egress", "protocol", "all", "matchall", "action", "drop")
+}
+
+// UnblockEgress removes a BlockEgress filter, restoring the named path's egress.
+// Idempotent — deleting an already-absent clsact qdisc is tolerated — so a test may
+// call it both explicitly and via t.Cleanup without failing on the second call.
+func (top *Topology) UnblockEgress(name string) {
+	p := top.path(name)
+	_ = top.tryRun("tc", "qdisc", "del", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
+}
+
 // Readdress replaces the edge-side address of the named path, simulating an edge
 // public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4
 // address in the path's /24.
diff --git a/test/e2e/standby_liveness_test.go b/test/e2e/standby_liveness_test.go
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e9230ed
--- /dev/null
+++ b/test/e2e/standby_liveness_test.go
@@ -0,0 +1,263 @@
+//go:build e2e
+
+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"strconv"
+	"strings"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/metrics"
+)
+
+// T104 (Q39, goals:G6) is an IN-GOAL VERIFICATION task, not a refactor: it asks
+// whether an idle-but-"up" standby path's liveness is genuinely BIDIRECTIONAL, or
+// whether it can be satisfied by receive-only traffic. The motivating production
+// observation was wanbond_path_up{path="5g"}=1 with wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total{
+// path="5g"}=0 — a standby path reported healthy while its tx counter never moved.
+// Two independent checks:
+//
+//   - standby-transmits-when-idle: an UP standby that carries no DATA (active-backup
+//     collapses all DATA onto the primary) must still be observed TRANSMITTING —
+//     its own periodic liveness probes are real wire writes (bind.emitProbes ->
+//     conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort) — while the primary carries a live flow.
+//   - standby-egress-blocked-goes-down: with the standby's EGRESS direction (only)
+//     blocked one-way at the edge veth (BlockEgress, a tc clsact/matchall/drop
+//     filter — see netns.go), the standby must transition DOWN and must NOT be
+//     selected when the primary is then killed. This is the affirmative half: it
+//     proves the liveness verdict requires THIS path's own send-probe/receive-echo
+//     round trip, not merely "traffic arrived on this interface" (the peer's own
+//     probes keep ARRIVING at the blocked path the whole time — see
+//     internal/bind/multipath.go dispatchInbound: an unechoed inbound PROBE only
+//     learns the remote and is reflected; it never marks OUR liveness up — only
+//     HandleEcho, driven by OUR OWN probe's authenticated echo, can).
+//
+// This harness cannot execute the `-tags e2e` netns tier itself (it requires
+// CAP_NET_ADMIN/root and runs via the dedicated privileged target — see AGENTS.md);
+// it is written to COMPILE and to make BOTH outcomes (pass, or a documented failure
+// kept as a defect repro per Q39) observable once run there. A static read of the
+// send path (internal/bind/probe.go's emitProbes never calls ps.txBytes.Add — only
+// Send() and fecFlushDeadline() in internal/bind/multipath.go do) predicts the first
+// subtest fails against the current implementation, reproducing the production
+// observation; that is an inference from source, not a measured result — the
+// authoritative answer is the hardware run's PASS/FAIL, at which point a genuine
+// failure should be refiled as a defect linked to goals:G6 with this test kept as
+// the repro, per the Q39 either-outcome acceptance.
+const (
+	t104MetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9100"
+	t104MetricsURL    = "http://" + t104MetricsListen + "/metrics"
+
+	// t104ProbeWindow is the idle-standby observation window for the tx-growth
+	// check: long enough (15 probe intervals at the default 200ms cadence) that
+	// even ONE counted probe would show up as a nonzero delta, well clear of
+	// scrape/scheduling jitter.
+	t104ProbeWindow = 15 * PLivenessProbeInterval
+)
+
+// TestStandbyLivenessBidirectional runs both T104 checks. Each brings up its own
+// topology (sequentially — the fixture's fixed veth names forbid two live
+// topologies), mirroring the subtest structure of TestP2Aggregation.
+func TestStandbyLivenessBidirectional(t *testing.T) {
+	bin := buildWanbond(t)
+
+	t.Run("standby-transmits-when-idle", func(t *testing.T) {
+		testStandbyIdleTransmits(t, bin)
+	})
+	t.Run("standby-egress-blocked-goes-down", func(t *testing.T) {
+		testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown(t, bin)
+	})
+}
+
+// testStandbyIdleTransmits is the FIRST T104 check: with the primary (starlink)
+// carrying a live flow and the standby (cellular) carrying no DATA (active-backup
+// default), the standby's wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total must still grow over a
+// multi-probe-interval window — its own periodic liveness probes are genuine wire
+// writes even though no DATA rides them.
+func testStandbyIdleTransmits(t *testing.T, bin string) {
+	t.Helper()
+	top := Setup(t)
+	edge, conc := setupT104Tunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, "error")
+
+	if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
+	}
+
+	primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
+	standby := DefaultPaths[backupPathIdx]  // cellular
+
+	waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 1, 10*time.Second)
+
+	// Drive a live flow on the primary for (at least) the whole observation window;
+	// active-backup keeps ALL of it off the standby, so the standby's own tx (if
+	// any is counted) can only come from its periodic probes.
+	loadSecs := int(t104ProbeWindow.Seconds()) + 10
+	top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
+	time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
+	top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(loadSecs))
+	time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond) // let the flow ramp before sampling the window
+
+	before := scrapeMetrics(t, t104MetricsURL)
+	txBefore, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
+	if !ok {
+		t.Fatalf("edge /metrics missing %s{path=%q}", metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
+	}
+	if up, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, standby.name); !ok || up != 1 {
+		t.Fatalf("standby %q not up at window start (up=%v ok=%v)\n%s", standby.name, up, ok, edge.log())
+	}
+	if up, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, primary.name); !ok || up != 1 {
+		t.Fatalf("primary %q not up at window start (up=%v ok=%v)\n%s", primary.name, up, ok, edge.log())
+	}
+
+	time.Sleep(t104ProbeWindow)
+
+	after := scrapeMetrics(t, t104MetricsURL)
+	txAfter, ok := after.PathValue(metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
+	if !ok {
+		t.Fatalf("edge /metrics missing %s{path=%q} on the second scrape", metrics.MetricTxBytes, standby.name)
+	}
+	if up, ok := after.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, standby.name); !ok || up != 1 {
+		t.Fatalf("standby %q dropped from up during the idle window (up=%v ok=%v) — cannot judge idle-transmit liveness while down\n%s",
+			standby.name, up, ok, edge.log())
+	}
+
+	delta := txAfter - txBefore
+	t.Logf("standby %q: wanbond_path_up=1 throughout a %s window with the primary carrying a live flow, tx delta = %.0f bytes",
+		standby.name, t104ProbeWindow, delta)
+	if delta <= 0 {
+		t.Errorf("standby path %q stayed wanbond_path_up=1 for the whole %s window while the primary carried a live "+
+			"flow, but its %s did not grow (delta=%.0f bytes) — this reproduces the production observation "+
+			"path_up{%s}=1 with tx{%s}=0. The standby's own periodic liveness probes ARE written to the wire "+
+			"(internal/bind/probe.go emitProbes -> conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort), but that write never increments "+
+			"ps.txBytes — only Send()/fecFlushDeadline() DATA/PARITY writes do (internal/bind/multipath.go). The "+
+			"path genuinely transmits; the operator-facing tx_bytes series just does not reflect it. Refile as a "+
+			"defect linked to goals:G6 (Q39) and keep this test as the reproduction.",
+			standby.name, t104ProbeWindow, metrics.MetricTxBytes, delta, standby.name, standby.name)
+	}
+}
+
+// testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown is the SECOND T104 check: with the standby's
+// egress (only) blocked one-way, it must transition DOWN — proving liveness needs
+// this path's own probe/echo round trip, not merely inbound traffic arriving on it
+// — and, once the primary is then killed, it must NOT be selected for failover.
+func testStandbyEgressBlockedGoesDown(t *testing.T, bin string) {
+	t.Helper()
+	top := Setup(t)
+	// "info" so the scheduler's "active path change" transitions are readable from
+	// the edge log, the same idiom TestP1Failover uses.
+	edge, conc := setupT104Tunnel(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, "info")
+
+	if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
+	}
+
+	primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
+	standby := DefaultPaths[backupPathIdx]  // cellular
+
+	waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 1, 10*time.Second)
+
+	// Block ONLY the standby's egress at the edge: outbound frames on its veth are
+	// dropped while the veth stays up and inbound (the concentrator's own probes)
+	// keeps arriving. A receive-only liveness check would stay wrongly up here.
+	top.BlockEgress(standby.name)
+	t.Cleanup(func() { top.UnblockEgress(standby.name) })
+
+	waitPathUp(t, t104MetricsURL, standby.name, 0, PLivenessDetectBudget)
+	t.Logf("standby %q went DOWN within %s of its egress being blocked one-way (its own probe/echo round trip broke; inbound traffic alone did not keep it up)",
+		standby.name, PLivenessDetectBudget)
+
+	// Force a failover decision: kill the still-healthy primary. If the standby's
+	// DOWN verdict is genuine, active-backup has no healthy path left, so egress
+	// must NOT reroute onto the standby and connectivity must NOT recover.
+	top.Blackhole(primary.name)
+	t.Cleanup(func() { top.Restore(primary.name) })
+
+	noFailoverWindow := PLivenessFailoverBudget + 3*time.Second
+	if top.pingUntil(concInner, noFailoverWindow) {
+		t.Errorf("connectivity recovered within %s after the primary died with the standby's egress still blocked "+
+			"— the falsely-live standby was selected for failover\n--- edge ---\n%s", noFailoverWindow, edge.log())
+	} else {
+		t.Logf("connectivity correctly did NOT recover within %s — the down standby was not selected", noFailoverWindow)
+	}
+	if idx := currentActivePathIdx(edge.log()); idx == backupPathIdx {
+		t.Errorf("edge scheduler switched its active path to the standby (index %d, %q) despite its egress being "+
+			"blocked and its liveness DOWN\n%s", backupPathIdx, standby.name, edge.log())
+	}
+
+	// Sanity: the fixture recovers once both faults are cleared — a genuine
+	// liveness-driven exclusion, not a wedged/broken harness.
+	top.UnblockEgress(standby.name)
+	top.Restore(primary.name)
+	if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("bond did not recover after clearing the egress block and restoring the primary\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
+			edge.log(), conc.log())
+	}
+}
+
+// setupT104Tunnel brings the multipath tunnel up over paths with the (default,
+// omitted) active-backup scheduler and the /metrics endpoint enabled on the edge —
+// only the edge's tx/up series are asserted by either T104 check, so only the edge
+// carries the [metrics] block. It otherwise mirrors setupMultipathTunnelLevel.
+func setupT104Tunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, level string) (edge, conc *proc) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
+	concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
+	psk := randKey(t)
+
+	var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
+	for _, p := range paths {
+		fmt.Fprintf(&edgePaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
+		fmt.Fprintf(&concPaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
+	}
+	primary := paths[0]
+	metricsBlock := fmt.Sprintf("[metrics]\nlisten = %q\n\n", t104MetricsListen)
+
+	dir := t.TempDir()
+	edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
+psk = "%s"
+
+%s%s[wireguard]
+private_key = "%s"
+
+[[wireguard.peers]]
+public_key = "%s"
+endpoint = "%s:%d"
+allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
+
+[log]
+level = %q
+`, psk, edgePaths.String(), metricsBlock, edgePriv, concPub, primary.concIP, listenPort, concInner, level))
+
+	concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
+psk = "%s"
+
+%s[wireguard]
+private_key = "%s"
+listen_port = %d
+
+[[wireguard.peers]]
+public_key = "%s"
+allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
+
+[log]
+level = %q
+`, psk, concPaths.String(), concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner, level))
+
+	conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
+	edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
+
+	if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
+	}
+	if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
+	}
+	top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
+	top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
+	top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
+	top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
+	return edge, conc
+}

+// BlockEgress drops ALL outbound traffic on the named path's edge veth via a tc
+// clsact egress filter (matchall + drop), while leaving the veth up and inbound
+// traffic untouched — a one-way egress failure (e.g. a dead uplink amplifier), as
+// opposed to Blackhole's bidirectional link-down. It is used by T104 to prove
+// liveness is genuinely bidirectional: with only egress dead the peer's own probes
+// still ARRIVE at this path (an operator naively trusting "receiving traffic" as
+// health would stay wrongly up), but this path's OWN probe/echo round trip cannot
+// complete, so it must go DOWN. clsact attaches its own ingress/egress hooks
+// independent of whatever root qdisc is set, so it coexists with the path's netem
+// root qdisc (delay/jitter/loss) without disturbing it.
+func (top *Topology) BlockEgress(name string) {
+	p := top.path(name)
+	top.run("tc", "qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
+	top.run("tc", "filter", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "egress", "protocol", "all", "matchall", "action", "drop")
+}
+
+// UnblockEgress removes a BlockEgress filter, restoring the named path's egress.
+// Idempotent — deleting an already-absent clsact qdisc is tolerated — so a test may
+// call it both explicitly and via t.Cleanup without failing on the second call.
+func (top *Topology) UnblockEgress(name string) {
+	p := top.path(name)
+	_ = top.tryRun("tc", "qdisc", "del", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
+}
+

 // public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4
 // address in the path's /24.

+// T104 (Q39, goals:G6) is an IN-GOAL VERIFICATION task, not a refactor: it asks
+// whether an idle-but-"up" standby path's liveness is genuinely BIDIRECTIONAL, or
+// whether it can be satisfied by receive-only traffic. The motivating production
+// observation was wanbond_path_up{path="5g"}=1 with wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total{
+// path="5g"}=0 — a standby path reported healthy while its tx counter never moved.
+// Two independent checks:
+//
+//   - standby-transmits-when-idle: an UP standby that carries no DATA (active-backup
+//     collapses all DATA onto the primary) must still be observed TRANSMITTING —
+//     its own periodic liveness probes are real wire writes (bind.emitProbes ->
+//     conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort) — while the primary carries a live flow.
+//   - standby-egress-blocked-goes-down: with the standby's EGRESS direction (only)
+//     blocked one-way at the edge veth (BlockEgress, a tc clsact/matchall/drop

+//     selected when the primary is then killed. This is the affirmative half: it

+//     internal/bind/multipath.go dispatchInbound: an unechoed inbound PROBE only

+psk = "%s"
+
+%s%s[wireguard]

+
+%s[wireguard]

+		t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
+	}
+	if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
+		t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
+	}
+	top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
+	top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")

1	//go:build e2e
2	
3	package e2e
4	
5	import (
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"os/exec"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	)
14	
15	// pathSpec describes one emulated WAN uplink between the edge and concentrator
16	// namespaces: a veth pair carrying a /24, with netem delay+jitter on the edge
17	// egress. The bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and controlled loss (lossPct) are
18	// OPTIONAL config-time impairments: both default to zero, so the DefaultPaths
19	// topology stays uncapped and lossless and every existing P0/P1 e2e test runs
20	// unchanged. A non-zero rateMbit makes the LINK — not the single-core userspace
21	// WG crypto — the bottleneck ONLY when rateMbit sits below the EXECUTING host's
22	// measured in-fixture tunnel ceiling. That ceiling is CPU/PPS-bound (both daemons
23	// plus the load generator share the host's cores), a lower bound that scales with
24	// core count, NOT a link-throughput spec: ~12–46 Mbit/s single-flow on a 1-vCPU
25	// aarch64 host (docs/p0-findings.md:216-225), ~13 Mbit/s single-path (up to
26	// ~47–87 Mbit/s FEC single-flow) on a 4-vCPU amd64 host. Sizing rule: cap < ceiling
27	// for single-path, 2×cap < ceiling for aggregation. Below the ceiling
28	// a standing queue can form for bufferbloat/pacing (T21/T23) work; a non-zero
29	// lossPct injects uniform egress loss at Setup time for FEC-recovery (T25/T29)
30	// work.
31	type pathSpec struct {
32		name     string
33		edgeIP   string
34		concIP   string
35		edgeVeth string // <=15 chars
36		concVeth string
37		delayMs  int
38		jitterMs int
39		rateMbit int     // optional per-path bandwidth cap (netem rate); 0 = uncapped
40		lossPct  float64 // optional config-time uniform egress loss (netem loss); 0 = lossless
41	
42		// deferEdgeAddr, when true, makes Setup create and bring up the edge veth WITHOUT
43		// assigning edgeIP to it: the interface exists (so tc/netem still applies and the
44		// link is up) but the configured source_addr is not yet owned by any interface —
45		// the well-formed-but-not-yet-assignable condition T51's tolerant Open() defers and
46		// T55's background reconcile later promotes (T60). AddEdgeAddr adds the withheld
47		// address later. Zero value (false) is the default for every existing path, so
48		// Setup's behaviour for DefaultPaths and every other caller is unchanged.
49		deferEdgeAddr bool
50	}
51	
52	// DefaultPaths are the two emulated links: Starlink-like (low latency, jittery)
53	// and 5G-like (higher latency, stable).
54	var DefaultPaths = []pathSpec{
55		{name: "starlink", edgeIP: "10.100.1.1", concIP: "10.100.1.2", edgeVeth: "wbAe", concVeth: "wbAc", delayMs: 45, jitterMs: 10},
56		{name: "cellular", edgeIP: "10.100.2.1", concIP: "10.100.2.2", edgeVeth: "wbBe", concVeth: "wbBc", delayMs: 64, jitterMs: 0},
57	}
58	
59	// Topology is a two-namespace netns/netem fixture. The edge side is the current
60	// (re-exec'd) network namespace; the concentrator side is a child process's
61	// network namespace, addressed by PID so no writable /run/netns is required.
62	type Topology struct {
63		t      *testing.T
64		holder *exec.Cmd
65		pid    int
66		paths  []pathSpec
67	}
68	
69	// Setup builds the two-path topology from DefaultPaths (uncapped, lossless). It
70	// requires CAP_NET_ADMIN, which the e2e TestMain provides via an unprivileged
71	// user+net namespace (`unshare -Urmn`).
72	func Setup(t *testing.T) *Topology {
73		return SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
74	}
75	
76	// SetupWithPaths builds the topology from an explicit path set, allowing a test
77	// to opt into the optional per-path bandwidth cap (rateMbit) and/or config-time
78	// loss (lossPct). DefaultPaths leaves both zero, so Setup is unchanged.
79	func SetupWithPaths(t *testing.T, paths []pathSpec) *Topology {
80		t.Helper()
81		top := &Topology{t: t, paths: paths}
82	
83		// Hold the concentrator network namespace open with a sleeping child.
84		top.holder = exec.Command("unshare", "-n", "sleep", "600")
85		if err := top.holder.Start(); err != nil {
86			t.Fatalf("start concentrator netns holder: %v", err)
87		}
88		top.pid = top.holder.Process.Pid
89		top.waitForNetns()
90	
91		pid := strconv.Itoa(top.pid)
92		top.run("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
93		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up")
94		// Pin net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the edge (test-process) network namespace, so
95		// a path whose source_addr is NOT assigned to any interface deterministically fails
96		// to bind (EADDRNOTAVAIL) instead of succeeding. That non-local-bind FAILURE is the
97		// premise the tolerant-startup tests (T60) rest on: a not-yet-assignable source_addr
98		// must DEFER (T51) and a config where NO path can bind must fail Open. A host that
99		// leaves the sysctl at 1 (or runs permissively) lets a non-local bind SUCCEED, which
100		// silently voids both premises (observed nondeterministic on a real host: a deferred
101		// path binding instead of deferring, or a zero-bindable daemon coming up). A fresh
102		// netns SHOULD default to 0, but pin it explicitly rather than trust the default.
103		// This affects ONLY non-local binds, so every other e2e test — which binds real,
104		// assigned addresses — is byte-for-byte unaffected. The concentrator netns needs no
105		// pin: every concentrator source_addr (concIP) IS assigned there (deferEdgeAddr only
106		// withholds the EDGE address), so the concentrator never binds a non-local address.
107		disableNonlocalBind(top.t)
108		for _, p := range top.paths {
109			// Idempotent pre-delete: the veth names are FIXED per path, so a prior subtest's
110			// teardown racing the kernel's async netns/veth reap can leave the edge veth behind,
111			// making `ip link add` fail with EEXIST ("File exists"). Deleting the pair first
112			// (ignore-if-absent) makes Setup robust to reused fixed names across sequential
113			// subtests. Deleting the edge end removes its peer too.
114			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
115			top.run("ip", "link", "add", p.edgeVeth, "type", "veth", "peer", "name", p.concVeth)
116			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "netns", pid)
117			if !p.deferEdgeAddr {
118				top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
119			}
120			top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
121			top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", p.concIP+"/24", "dev", p.concVeth)
122			top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", p.concVeth, "up")
123			qargs := append([]string{"qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
124			top.run("tc", qargs...)
125		}
126		t.Cleanup(top.Teardown)
127		return top
128	}
129	
130	// waitForNetns blocks until the holder's network namespace is observable.
131	func (top *Topology) waitForNetns() {
132		path := fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/ns/net", top.pid)
133		for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
134			if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
135				return
136			}
137			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
138		}
139		top.t.Fatalf("concentrator netns %s never appeared", path)
140	}
141	
142	// netemArgs builds the netem parameter list for a path's baseline impairment
143	// profile: delay/jitter, then its configured loss (lossPct) and bandwidth cap
144	// (rateMbit) if set. For a DefaultPaths entry (both zero) this reduces to the
145	// prior delay/jitter-only output, so existing qdisc setup is byte-identical.
146	func (top *Topology) netemArgs(p pathSpec) []string {
147		return top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, p.lossPct)
148	}
149	
150	// netemArgsWithLoss is netemArgs with the loss percentage overridden — used by
151	// InjectLoss to set runtime loss while preserving delay/jitter and any rate cap.
152	// netem accepts the options in delay/loss/rate order.
153	func (top *Topology) netemArgsWithLoss(p pathSpec, lossPct float64) []string {
154		args := []string{"delay", fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.delayMs)}
155		if p.jitterMs > 0 {
156			args = append(args, fmt.Sprintf("%dms", p.jitterMs))
157		}
158		if lossPct > 0 {
159			args = append(args, "loss", fmt.Sprintf("%g%%", lossPct))
160		}
161		if p.rateMbit > 0 {
162			args = append(args, "rate", fmt.Sprintf("%dmbit", p.rateMbit))
163		}
164		return args
165	}
166	
167	// path looks up a path by name, failing the test if absent.
168	func (top *Topology) path(name string) pathSpec {
169		for _, p := range top.paths {
170			if p.name == name {
171				return p
172			}
173		}
174		top.t.Fatalf("unknown path %q", name)
175		return pathSpec{}
176	}
177	
178	// RTT pings the concentrator end of the named path from the edge and returns the
179	// average round-trip time in milliseconds.
180	func (top *Topology) RTT(name string, count int) float64 {
181		p := top.path(name)
182		out := top.runOut("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP)
183		// rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 37.264/48.520/82.043/19.354 ms
184		idx := strings.Index(out, "min/avg/max")
185		if idx < 0 {
186			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: no rtt line in ping output:\n%s", name, out)
187		}
188		eq := strings.Index(out[idx:], "=")
189		fields := strings.Fields(out[idx+eq+1:])
190		nums := strings.Split(fields[0], "/")
191		avg, err := strconv.ParseFloat(nums[1], 64)
192		if err != nil {
193			top.t.Fatalf("path %q: parse avg rtt %q: %v", name, nums[1], err)
194		}
195		return avg
196	}
197	
198	// Reachable reports whether the concentrator end of the named path answers pings.
199	func (top *Topology) Reachable(name string, count int) bool {
200		p := top.path(name)
201		return top.tryRun("ping", "-c", strconv.Itoa(count), "-i", "0.2", "-W", "1", p.concIP) == nil
202	}
203	
204	// InjectLoss sets uniform egress loss (percent) on the named path at runtime,
205	// preserving its delay/jitter profile and any configured bandwidth cap.
206	func (top *Topology) InjectLoss(name string, pct float64) {
207		p := top.path(name)
208		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgsWithLoss(p, pct)...)
209		top.run("tc", args...)
210	}
211	
212	// ClearLoss restores the named path to its configured baseline impairment
213	// (delay/jitter, plus any config-time loss and bandwidth cap). For a DefaultPaths
214	// entry that is delay/jitter only.
215	func (top *Topology) ClearLoss(name string) {
216		p := top.path(name)
217		args := append([]string{"qdisc", "change", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "root", "netem"}, top.netemArgs(p)...)
218		top.run("tc", args...)
219	}
220	
221	// Blackhole brings the named path's edge link down, simulating a WAN death (used
222	// by the P1 failover test).
223	func (top *Topology) Blackhole(name string) {
224		p := top.path(name)
225		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "down")
226	}
227	
228	// Restore brings the named path's edge link back up.
229	func (top *Topology) Restore(name string) {
230		p := top.path(name)
231		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
232	}
233	
234	// BlockEgress drops ALL outbound traffic on the named path's edge veth via a tc
235	// clsact egress filter (matchall + drop), while leaving the veth up and inbound
236	// traffic untouched — a one-way egress failure (e.g. a dead uplink amplifier), as
237	// opposed to Blackhole's bidirectional link-down. It is used by T104 to prove
238	// liveness is genuinely bidirectional: with only egress dead the peer's own probes
239	// still ARRIVE at this path (an operator naively trusting "receiving traffic" as
240	// health would stay wrongly up), but this path's OWN probe/echo round trip cannot
241	// complete, so it must go DOWN. clsact attaches its own ingress/egress hooks
242	// independent of whatever root qdisc is set, so it coexists with the path's netem
243	// root qdisc (delay/jitter/loss) without disturbing it.
244	func (top *Topology) BlockEgress(name string) {
245		p := top.path(name)
246		top.run("tc", "qdisc", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
247		top.run("tc", "filter", "add", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "egress", "protocol", "all", "matchall", "action", "drop")
248	}
249	
250	// UnblockEgress removes a BlockEgress filter, restoring the named path's egress.
251	// Idempotent — deleting an already-absent clsact qdisc is tolerated — so a test may
252	// call it both explicitly and via t.Cleanup without failing on the second call.
253	func (top *Topology) UnblockEgress(name string) {
254		p := top.path(name)
255		_ = top.tryRun("tc", "qdisc", "del", "dev", p.edgeVeth, "clsact")
256	}
257	
258	// Readdress replaces the edge-side address of the named path, simulating an edge
259	// public-IP change (used by the P1 roaming test). newEdgeIP must be a bare IPv4
260	// address in the path's /24.
261	func (top *Topology) Readdress(name, newEdgeIP string) {
262		p := top.path(name)
263		top.run("ip", "addr", "flush", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
264		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", newEdgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
265		top.run("ip", "link", "set", p.edgeVeth, "up")
266	}
267	
268	// AddEdgeAddr adds the named path's configured edge-side address to its (already up)
269	// edge veth, WITHOUT flushing any existing address first (T60). It is the companion
270	// to a path built with deferEdgeAddr: that path's interface starts addressless, so
271	// this simulates the address becoming assignable after boot (e.g. a 5G modem's DHCP
272	// lease completing) for the T55 background reconciler to observe. Unlike Readdress —
273	// which flushes THEN re-adds, for the T16 re-roam case where an address is replaced —
274	// this only ADDS, because here there is nothing to flush.
275	func (top *Topology) AddEdgeAddr(name string) {
276		p := top.path(name)
277		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", p.edgeIP+"/24", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
278	}
279	
280	// nonlocalBindProc is the per-netns sysctl controlling whether a socket may bind a
281	// source address that is NOT assigned to any local interface. 0 (the value the
282	// tolerant-startup tests pin) makes such a bind fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; 1 lets it
283	// succeed. It is a plain proc file, so it is written/read directly rather than via
284	// the `sysctl` binary (one fewer external dependency).
285	const nonlocalBindProc = "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_nonlocal_bind"
286	
287	// disableNonlocalBind pins ip_nonlocal_bind=0 in the CURRENT (edge/test) network
288	// namespace and asserts the value took, failing the test loudly if it cannot be set.
289	// A hard failure here — rather than proceeding — is deliberate: the tolerant-startup
290	// premise (a non-local bind must fail) is silently voided if the sysctl stays at 1,
291	// so an un-pinnable environment must surface as a test failure, never a false pass.
292	// os.WriteFile targets THIS process's netns, which is the edge/test namespace (the
293	// TestMain re-exec unshared it), exactly where the not-yet-assignable source_addr
294	// binds live. Writing a per-netns net sysctl needs the same privilege the fixture's
295	// veth/netem setup already requires, so anywhere the netns tier runs, this succeeds.
296	func disableNonlocalBind(t *testing.T) {
297		t.Helper()
298		if err := os.WriteFile(nonlocalBindProc, []byte("0\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
299			t.Fatalf("pin %s=0: %v (the tolerant-startup tests need a non-local bind to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL; without this a permissive host silently voids the premise)", nonlocalBindProc, err)
300		}
301		got, err := os.ReadFile(nonlocalBindProc)
302		if err != nil {
303			t.Fatalf("read back %s: %v", nonlocalBindProc, err)
304		}
305		if v := strings.TrimSpace(string(got)); v != "0" {
306			t.Fatalf("%s = %q after pin, want 0 — a non-local bind would still succeed and void the zero-bindable/deferred premise", nonlocalBindProc, v)
307		}
308	}
309	
310	// bringLoopbackUp brings the loopback interface UP in the CURRENT (edge/test)
311	// network namespace. It is a second, INDEPENDENT precondition for a non-local bind
312	// to fail EADDRNOTAVAIL: on this kernel the rejection fires only when at least one
313	// interface is UP — empirically, with lo DOWN a bind to a non-local address SUCCEEDS
314	// even at ip_nonlocal_bind=0 (kernel probe: lo-up+pin0 -> EADDRNOTAVAIL; lo-down+pin0
315	// -> BIND_OK; lo-up+pin1 -> BIND_OK). So the zero-bindable premise needs BOTH lo UP
316	// and the sysctl pinned to 0. SetupWithPaths already brings lo up for topology tests
317	// (line above), which is why the zero-bindable subtest passed only when it happened to
318	// run AFTER one; building no topology itself, it must bring lo up explicitly to hold
319	// regardless of subtest execution order. Idempotent (setting an already-up lo up is a
320	// no-op). Runs in this process's netns (the TestMain re-exec unshared it), like run().
321	func bringLoopbackUp(t *testing.T) {
322		t.Helper()
323		if out, err := exec.Command("ip", "link", "set", "lo", "up").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
324			t.Fatalf("bring lo up in the test netns: %v\n%s", err, out)
325		}
326	}
327	
328	// QdiscShow returns `tc qdisc show` for the named path (for assertions/debug).
329	func (top *Topology) QdiscShow(name string) string {
330		p := top.path(name)
331		return top.runOut("tc", "qdisc", "show", "dev", p.edgeVeth)
332	}
333	
334	// LinkExists reports whether the edge-side veth of the named path still exists.
335	func (top *Topology) LinkExists(name string) bool {
336		p := top.path(name)
337		return top.tryRun("ip", "link", "show", p.edgeVeth) == nil
338	}
339	
340	// Teardown removes the topology. Killing the holder drops the concentrator netns
341	// (and its veth ends); the edge-side veths are deleted explicitly. Idempotent.
342	func (top *Topology) Teardown() {
343		for _, p := range top.paths {
344			_ = top.tryRun("ip", "link", "del", p.edgeVeth)
345		}
346		if top.holder != nil && top.holder.Process != nil {
347			_ = top.holder.Process.Kill()
348			_, _ = top.holder.Process.Wait()
349			top.holder = nil
350		}
351	}
352	
353	func (top *Topology) run(name string, args ...string) {
354		top.t.Helper()
355		if out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
356			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
357		}
358	}
359	
360	func (top *Topology) tryRun(name string, args ...string) error {
361		return exec.Command(name, args...).Run()
362	}
363	
364	func (top *Topology) runOut(name string, args ...string) string {
365		top.t.Helper()
366		out, err := exec.Command(name, args...).CombinedOutput()
367		if err != nil {
368			top.t.Fatalf("%s %s: %v\n%s", name, strings.Join(args, " "), err, out)
369		}
370		return string(out)
371	}
372	
373	// iperfListenTimeout bounds waitIperfListen: how long to wait for the one-shot
374	// iperf3 server to reach the LISTEN state before failing the test.
375	const iperfListenTimeout = 5 * time.Second
376	
377	// waitIperfListen polls, inside the concentrator network namespace, for a TCP
378	// socket in the LISTEN state on the given port, and returns once it appears (or
379	// fails the test at iperfListenTimeout). It reads kernel socket state via
380	// `ss -ltn` and NEVER connects: the iperf3 servers here run one-shot (`-s -1`),
381	// so a probe-connect would consume the server's single accept and break the real
382	// client. It replaces the prior fixed sleeps at the iperf3Mbps and rttUnderLoad
383	// call sites, which raced a slow bind under load into 'connection refused' (D3).
384	func (top *Topology) waitIperfListen(t *testing.T, port int) {
385		t.Helper()
386		suffix := fmt.Sprintf(":%d", port)
387		deadline := time.Now().Add(iperfListenTimeout)
388		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
389			// ss -ltn columns: State Recv-Q Send-Q Local-Address:Port Peer-Address:Port.
390			out, err := exec.Command("nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "ss", "-ltn").CombinedOutput()
391			if err == nil {
392				for _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), "\n") {
393					fields := strings.Fields(line)
394					if len(fields) >= 4 && strings.HasSuffix(fields[3], suffix) {
395						return
396					}
397				}
398			}
399			time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
400		}
401		t.Fatalf("iperf3 server never reached LISTEN on port %d within %s", port, iperfListenTimeout)
402	}
403	
404	// nsenter runs a command inside the concentrator network namespace.
405	func (top *Topology) nsenter(args ...string) {
406		top.t.Helper()
407		full := append([]string{"-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n"}, args...)
408		top.run("nsenter", full...)
409	}

1	package bind
2	
3	import (
4		"sync"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/7mind/wanbond/internal/telemetry"
8	)
9	
10	// emitProbes performs one probe cadence step: for every currently-open path it
11	// emits one authenticated PROBE frame (IsEcho=false) to that path's learned/
12	// configured remote, then Ticks that path's Prober so liveness advances against
13	// the injected clock. A path without a known remote yet is still Ticked (so a
14	// silent path is detected Down) but nothing is sent — there is nowhere to send.
15	//
16	// Concurrency mirrors Send: the path/prober set is snapshotted under m.mu, then
17	// released before any socket I/O, so emission neither holds the lock across a
18	// syscall nor blocks the lock-free receive fast path. A concurrent Close closes
19	// the sockets out from under the snapshot; the resulting write error is benign
20	// (teardown) and dropped. It is a no-op when the bind has no probers or is closed.
21	func (m *Multipath) emitProbes() {
22		m.mu.Lock()
23		if len(m.paths) == 0 || m.probers == nil {
24			m.mu.Unlock()
25			return
26		}
27		type target struct {
28			ps *peerPathState
29			pr *telemetry.Prober
30		}
31		targets := make([]target, 0, len(m.paths))
32		for _, ps := range m.paths {
33			if ps.prober == nil {
34				continue
35			}
36			targets = append(targets, target{ps: ps, pr: ps.prober})
37		}
38		m.mu.Unlock()
39	
40		for _, t := range targets {
41			if remote, ok := t.ps.getRemote(); ok {
42				if raw, err := t.pr.SendProbe(); err == nil {
43					// UDP writes are goroutine-safe; this races no in-flight Send.
44					_, _ = t.ps.conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort(raw, remote)
45				}
46			}
47			t.pr.Tick()
48		}
49		// Eager failover nudge (defect D18): recompute the scheduler's active egress path
50		// each probe cadence so a liveness DOWN switches egress even when no application
51		// Send is driving Pick. This is the timer-driven companion to the receive-tick
52		// nudge (see tickLivenessFromReceive) — under CPU starvation this loop's ticker
53		// lags, which is why the receive-tick path carries the guarantee, but when the
54		// loop does run it keeps the selection fresh independent of egress traffic.
55		m.nudgeSchedulerActive()
56	}
57	
58	// StartProbeLoop launches the probe cadence goroutine: it calls emitProbes every
59	// interval until the returned stop function is invoked. The caller (device.Up)
60	// starts it AFTER the engine has opened the bind and stops it BEFORE Close, so the
61	// loop only runs while the sockets exist (emitProbes is a safe no-op if it races a
62	// closed bind). The returned stopper is idempotent.
63	//
64	// The cadence uses a wall-clock ticker because it is production timing glue, not
65	// liveness logic: every liveness decision the loop drives runs through the
66	// injected telemetry.Clock the Probers hold (SendProbe stamps it, Tick reads it),
67	// so tests drive emitProbes directly against a fake clock and never start this
68	// goroutine. It is a no-op (returning a no-op stopper) when the bind has no
69	// probers or interval <= 0.
70	func (m *Multipath) StartProbeLoop(interval time.Duration) (stop func()) {
71		if m.probers == nil || interval <= 0 {
72			return func() {}
73		}
74		// Arm the receive-path liveness sweep at the same cadence (D15): a receiver may
75		// now advance liveness when the timer goroutine below is starved under load.
76		m.sweepIntervalNanos.Store(int64(interval))
77		done := make(chan struct{})
78		go func() {
79			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
80			defer ticker.Stop()
81			for {
82				select {
83				case <-done:
84					return
85				case <-ticker.C:
86					m.emitProbes()
87				}
88			}
89		}()
90		var once sync.Once
91		return func() { once.Do(func() { close(done) }) }
92	}

HPID=$!

HPID=$(pgrep -x sleep | while read p; do [ "$(readlink /proc/$p/ns/net)" != "$(readlink /proc/$$/ns/net)" ] && echo $p; done | head -1)
echo "holder pid: $HPID"

nsenter -t $HPID -n ip addr add 10.9.9.2/24 dev vB
nsenter -t $HPID -n ip link set vB up

tc qdisc del dev vA clsact 2>/dev/null || echo IDEMPOTENT-DEL-TOLERATED
kill $HPID 2>/dev/null || true

operational step, update the affected docs **in the same change**:

- **[README.md](README.md)** — the front door: capabilities, quick start, repo
  layout, status/limitations. Update when a capability, config surface, package,
  or limitation changes.
- **[docs/design.md](docs/design.md)** — architecture and what we built on top of
  amneziawg-go. Update when the data path, a layer/package, an invariant, the
  security model, or a deliberate boundary changes.
- **[docs/install.md](docs/install.md)** — setup and operation. Update when
  config keys, systemd units, firewall/provisioning steps, or metrics change.
- **[docs/manual-checklist.md](docs/manual-checklist.md)** — update when a
  verification step or a real-link measurement changes.

A change that moves the code but not the docs it invalidates is **incomplete**.
If you are unsure whether a doc is affected, grep it for the symbol/keyword you
touched. Prefer editing the existing doc over adding a new one; keep docs
consistent with each other (e.g. a config key described in README, design, and
install must match).

tooling on `PATH`. Run tools from it (e.g. `nix develop --command bash -c '…'`).

content.text:
All checks complete. Verdict follows.

**Evidence gathered:**

**(a) BlockEgress mechanics — empirically verified.** I reproduced the exact fixture layout (veth pair, peer end in a separate netns via holder PID, `netem delay 5ms 2ms` root qdisc) in an unprivileged `unshare -Urmn` sandbox and ran the exact BlockEgress/UnblockEgress command sequences: baseline ping OK (~8ms rtt, netem active); after `clsact` + `egress protocol all matchall action drop`: outbound 100% loss while 3/3 inbound packets still arrived (RX delta = 3) — a genuine one-way egress block; `tc qdisc show` confirmed the netem root qdisc (same handle/seed) coexisted with clsact and survived the unblock intact; connectivity restored after `UnblockEgress`; the second delete was tolerated (idempotent, via `tryRun`). Note my first repro attempt was invalid (both veth ends in one netns → loopback short-circuit) — the cross-netns rerun is the authoritative one.

**(b) The tx-growth assertion reads the right counter.** Chain verified: `wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total` (`internal/metrics/metrics.go:24`) ← `internal/device/metrics.go` ← `Multipath.PathSnapshots()` (`internal/bind/multipath.go:2196`, reads `ps.txBytes`). If probes did increment `ps.txBytes`, the 3s window (15 × 200ms `telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval`) guarantees a positive delta. Conversely no spurious pass is possible: `Send` routes only to the scheduler-picked (primary) path, FEC is off, and the echo-reflection write is also uncounted — nothing else can grow the standby's tx.

**(c) The emitProbes omission is real — independently confirmed.** `ps.txBytes.Add` appears at exactly two sites: `internal/bind/multipath.go:1477` (Send) and `:1603` (fecFlushDeadline). `emitProbes` (`internal/bind/probe.go:44`) writes the probe via `conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort` with no accounting; so does the echo reflection in `dispatchInbound` (`multipath.go:~1284`). The counter doc (`multipath.go:140-151`) is explicitly asymmetric: tx "counts the DATA-frame wire bytes", rx counts "every outer datagram... DATA, PROBE, and echo alike" — while the exposition help string claims "Total bytes transmitted on the path". This asymmetry exactly explains the production `path_up=1 / tx=0` observation. Crucially it is a **metrics-accounting fault, not a liveness hole**: inbound non-echo PROBE only does `setRemote` + `Reflect` (`multipath.go:1265-1285`), never marks liveness up; only `HandleEcho` on our own probe's authenticated echo does; `tickLivenessFromReceive` only Ticks (advances down-detection). An egress-dead standby goes DOWN within DownAfter — subtest (b)'s premise holds statically, and the all-down scheduler case (`active_backup.go recomputeLocked`) yields `active=-1` ("no eligible path"), never the down standby, so the post-kill assertions cannot false-positive.

**(d) Gate green, idioms match.** Ran in the worktree via `nix develop`: `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...`, `gofmt -l` clean, `golangci-lint run --build-tags e2e` 0 issues, unit tests pass. All harness helpers exist with matching signatures; per-test metrics port (9100) follows the 9095-9099 convention; the 400ms post-server sleep matches P2's surviving `startSaturatingLoad` idiom (the D3 `waitIperfListen` fix applies to the connect-under-load call sites); budgets come from `thresholds.go`; `currentActivePathIdx` parses the `to` field including `-1`.

**Defect recommendation:** file now on static evidence — the source-level confirmation is conclusive and does not depend on the hardware run; the netns run will then bind subtest (a) to the defect as its repro per Q39.

### Session summary
- **Did:** reviewed task T104 round 1 (diff 55ff983..1d5d01e); empirically validated BlockEgress in a sandboxed netns replica; traced the tx-accounting chain through bind/device/metrics; ran the full non-privileged gate in the worktree
- **Achieved:** verdict approve; 0 criticisms / 0 questions / 1 defect filed for orchestrator
- **Discovered:** BlockEgress is a genuine one-way egress block coexisting with netem (verified by experiment); emitProbes and echo reflection bypass ps.txBytes while rxBytes counts all inbound — confirming the production path_up=1/tx=0 as a metrics omission, not a liveness hole; subtest (b)'s no-false-up premise verified in dispatchInbound/HandleEcho and the all-down scheduler path
- **Issues:** privileged netns tier not runnable here, per dispatch — pass/fail of the run itself remains to be observed on hardware; the worker's FAIL prediction for subtest (a) is source-consistent
